highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 brings performance improvements, a memory-leak fix, and Nano Server font support.
highlights_body: This release fixes a pixel-buffer memory leak in the SKImage raster failure path, reduces allocations in text encoding, speeds up SKRuntimeEffect uniform lookups, and adds FreeType-backed font support for Windows Nano Server.

bullets.lead: Pixel-buffer memory leak fixed in SKImage.Create
bullets.detail: A CoTaskMem-allocated pixel buffer leaked when SKImage.Create failed to create a raster image; the buffer is now correctly freed on the failure path.

bullets.lead: FreeType font support for Windows Nano Server
bullets.detail: SkiaSharp now uses FreeType as its font backend on Windows Nano Server, enabling text rendering in environments where GDI fonts are unavailable.
The null-terminated path in GetEncodedText no longer allocates an intermediate string, lowering GC pressure in text-heavy rendering workloads.
The uniform lookup map inside SKRuntimeEffectUniforms is now pre-sized to the known uniform count, eliminating repeated rehashing during effect evaluation.

contributor_summaries.jeffska: Added FreeType-backed font support for Windows Nano Server environments
